frameworkDiversity.description: Does it use MULTIPLE exploration frameworks (SCAMPER, Concept Reframe, Competitive, Constraint Play, User Journey) — not just SCAMPER?

scoring.10: Uses 3+ frameworks, each direction explores a fundamentally different angle
scoring.7: Uses 2 frameworks, most directions are meaningfully different
scoring.4: Uses only SCAMPER, variations are layout-level not concept-level
scoring.1: All directions use the same framework with minor tweaks

conceptDepth.description: Do directions explore CONCEPT-level variation (metaphors, mental models, interaction paradigms) — not just layout rearrangement?

scoring.10: At least 2 directions reframe the fundamental approach (different metaphor, different IA model, different interaction paradigm)
scoring.7: One direction reframes the concept, rest are layout variations
scoring.4: All directions rearrange the same content into different layouts
scoring.1: Directions are cosmetic shuffles (sidebar left vs right)

depthProgression.description: Does it offer concept cards first (cheap) before investing in sketches/builds (expensive)?

scoring.10: Starts with concept cards, lets user promote to sketches, then builds. Diverge cheap, converge expensive.
scoring.7: Offers depth choice but defaults to full build
scoring.4: Goes straight to full builds without concept-level exploration
scoring.1: No depth options — one mode only

thesisClarity.description: Does each direction have a clear, defensible thesis grounded in user behavior?

scoring.10: Each direction: 'This version prioritizes X because user Y needs Z' with specific behavioral reasoning
scoring.7: Thesis present but behavior reasoning is thin
scoring.4: Directions have names but no clear thesis

convergenceSupport.description: Does it offer structured merge/hybrid creation, not just 'pick A, B, or C'?

scoring.10: Offers merge with conflict detection: 'V2's sidebar nav conflicts with V3's top nav — which?'
scoring.7: Mentions merging but no structured process
scoring.4: Just 'pick one' with no merge option

recommendationQuality.description: Is the recommendation grounded in user behavior, not aesthetic preference?

scoring.10: 'Variation 2 works for ops teams because the status bar eliminates scrolling before knowing if something's broken'
scoring.7: Recommendation cites user behavior but could be more specific

buildReadiness.description: At full-build depth, does each variation produce plan.md + build.json + tasks.md?

scoring.10: Each full-build variation has all three files, tasks.md has literal text and resolved overrides
scoring.7: Plan.md and build.json present, tasks.md missing or incomplete
scoring.1: No buildable output — just text descriptions
